docs.title: Removing "assertions" from cb:a098
docs.description: Covers the rename of the dead term "assertions" to "beliefs" in cb:a098 and the mood fix it forces - the proposed directive D that supersedes a098's first clause.

docs.title: Generalize atomicity (cb:a475) to all four types
docs.description: Covers extending the primitive-only atomicity doctrine (cb:a475) to all belief types - one belief, one falsifiable proposition - which is what licenses splitting multi-claim nodes like a098.

docs.title: Quote sources verbatim alongside citations
docs.description: Covers the citation-discipline rule - quote the load-bearing source passage beside the pointer - minted as agent-behavior:a411. This doc is the focus that produced it.

docs.title: Commit provenance at the floor tier (threads, ledgers, briefs)
docs.description: Covers extending the graph tier's structural commit provenance (cb:c067 commit: scheme, Belief: trailers, mix cb.verify.commits, the cb:a563 todo gate) down to floor-tier lifecycle events - one commit per lifecycle event, Thread:/Focus: trailers as floor analogs of Belief:, back-pointers only with git as the sole index, and merge-without-squash as the SHA-durability condition. Deliberation open: trailer vocabulary, verify extension, and squash policy await resolution.

docs.title: Demote the contract boolean to a derived predicate
docs.description: Covers dropping the stored `contract` field - redundant with rules/invariants by c056's biconditional, zero-drift across 39 nodes, a a386-style stored derivation - so contract-grade-ness is computed on read; and collapsing the c059 "contracts may stand alone" carve-out that the demotion exposes as arbitrary, so every prescription grounds in deps or a stipulation artifact.

docs.title: Nursery
docs.description: Use when orienting to the belief nursery - the floor-tier workspace where focuses (proto-beliefs) are deliberated in place until they mint into the graph or are dropped.

docs.title: The mint manifest - typed decomposition inside the brief, not typed documents
docs.description: Covers the mint manifest - a late-stage section in a maturing focus brief enumerating the candidate beliefs it will plant as typed rows (structural type, draft claim, deps, grounding artifact, minted id) - adopted as the surviving weak form of the rejected typed-nursery-documents proposal; action items in briefs are prescription rows, resolving the focus/plan drift in composition with seed-lifecycle's collapse. Minted cb:a567.

docs.title: A negative-case schema field?
docs.description: Covers whether CB should add a negative-case field for the "X, not Y" contrast in claims - leaning no, because the negative channel already exists as invariants.

docs.title: The nursery architecture (focus-as-unit, Layer 1/2 fusion)
docs.description: Covers the nursery model itself - focus-as-unit, conversing directly in the artifact, making the raw transcript vestigial - and its conflict with the OKF transcript-pair rule.

docs.title: Per-belief file storage (split beliefs.json into one file per node)
docs.description: Covers the decision to store the graph as one JSON file per belief (a beliefs/cb/a512.json layout) instead of a single array - distinct from the per-entity grouping cb:a112 rejected - the worktree prototype that proved the read path, and the six-step implementation plan that mints from here.

docs.title: The routing ledger - per-thread dispatch for non-linear conversations
docs.description: Covers the routing ledger - a per-thread table of topics, strand states, dispatch pointers, and dangling questions that lets a multi-topic thread be resumed from its ledger instead of from memory - designed as a router, never a digest (content lands in focus docs; the ledger holds only pointers and states, keeping it outside cb:a386's reach); location lean in-thread, maintained by the hook or a /decompose skill. Minted cb:a566; open on hook mechanics and the skill build.

docs.title: Seed absorption - terminal seeds fold into their successor and evacuate
docs.description: Covers the principle that every terminal seed folds into its successor and evacuates the nursery - on planting into a `seed` prop on the belief, on losing a contest into the winning seed as dated historical evidence - so the nursery never holds a tombstone; plus the floor/graph tier split, the persist-raw safety condition, and the open granularity and field questions.

docs.title: Seed lifecycle - graduate, don't evacuate (and the seed/plan collapse)
docs.description: Covers whether terminal seeds persist by graduation (historicize and archive) instead of evacuating by deletion, and whether persisted seeds and plans collapse into one durable-brief concept - contesting seed-absorption's fold-and-evacuate and the no-tombstones-by-deletion reading of the nursery doctrine.

docs.title: Recency and dating for adjudicating competing seeds
docs.description: Covers datestamping seed docs (last-edited) and their thread excerpts so an agent can rank competing seed positions by recency - resolved as a soft prioritization hint, with explicit contested-links as the hard resolution; the frontmatter field is the open mechanic.

docs.title: Associate each thread statement with the artifact it feeds
docs.description: Covers linking each user/agent statement in a thread to the seed or belief it is synthesized into - the back-edge of transcript-format's seeds-carry-excerpts (seed to exchange), making transcript-to-graph traceability bidirectional at statement granularity.

docs.title: Rename the four structural types to nominalized epistemic acts
docs.description: Covers renaming the type enum from primitive/compound/inference/directive to attestation/aggregation/inference/prescription - nominalizations of c051's four verbs (attest/aggregate/infer/prescribe) - fixing the mixed shape-word/act-word register, why compound and inference cannot collapse, why prescription is preferred over actualization, and the layered vocabulary migration.

docs.title: Bind each thread to the repo it relates to (thread init)
docs.description: Covers persisting each thread doc in the repo the work concerns rather than always in composable-beliefs - set by a thread-init step at the start of a session, since the transcript hook currently hardcodes a single threads dir.

docs.title: 2026-06-25 - belief-by-belief audit (starting cb:a098)
docs.description: Covers the node-by-node belief audit opened on cb:a098 - its split into a directive plus inference, and the schema questions raised: generalizing atomicity past primitives, a negative-case field, mood living in kind. Use when revisiting a098 or those decisions.

docs.title: 2026-07-01 - schema-v3 execution (rename + demotion shipped)
docs.description: Covers the execution session for the structural-type vocabulary migration - the code-only compat-shim phase (PR #1), the authorized beliefs/ migration (220 type rewrites, contract field stripped, contract family rewritten, evidence recorded on c051/c056), the scope calls made in flight (history untouched, c059 carve-out kept, glossary render deferred), and the two follow-up prescriptions minted at close (cb:a561, cb:a562). Use when tracing the migration commits back to their seeds and decisions.

docs.title: 2026-07-01 - seed lifecycle deliberation (graduate vs evacuate)
docs.description: Covers the session that deliberated the seed-lifecycle brief - whether terminal seeds evacuate or persist, and whether seed and plan collapse - landing on scenario C and authoring the seed-lifecycle focus; plus the branch mechanics (merge, post-PR reset) and the thread-persistence exchange that led to hand-capturing this transcript. Use when revisiting the seed-lifecycle decision or the commit-the-threads question.

docs.title: 2026-07-01 - structural-type vocabulary (rename + contract demotion)
docs.description: Covers the design conversation that questioned the contract boolean and the primitive/compound/directive names, arriving at a four-type rename to nominalized epistemic acts, a demotion of the contract field to a derived predicate, the c059 carve-out that demotion exposes, and the decision to persist the work as nursery seeds rather than a separate plan. Use when revisiting the structural-type vocabulary or the seed-vs-plan question.

docs.title: 2026-07-02 - authoring pipeline (thread-to-graph round trip)
docs.description: Covers the session that designed the thread-to-graph authoring pipeline - the routing ledger, the mint manifest (typed decomposition inside the brief, rejecting typed nursery documents), concurrence with seed-lifecycle's collapse, and extending commit provenance to the floor tier - executed same-session as the first full round trip from thread capture to minted beliefs. Use when orienting to the authoring pipeline or auditing the round-trip reference.

docs.title: Threads (session transcripts)
docs.description: Use when you need the raw turn-by-turn transcript of a session - the human-readable, crash-safe record. Not provenance; the nursery seeds are.

docs.title: How transcripts and seeds persist exchanges
docs.description: Spike brief for the transcript/seed persistence pipeline - whether to commit the raw jsonl, generating metadata at /end, conforming belief-audit.md and the .sessions transcripts to one format, and seeds carrying per-topic excerpts. Self-contained; start the implementation thread here.
